Q1 2023 EPS $3.46 up sequentially from Q4 2022 $3.33, though revenue $50.79B down from Q3 2022 peak $66.64B reflecting oil price normalization
Buffett holds CVX as one of top 6 positions in $366B portfolio — quality/dividend endorsement
Stock lagged with +8.7% 1y return and -3.6% 3m, creating entry point; asset sales (NM/TX) signal portfolio optimization

MCD.rationale: Premium 31.9x P/E near 1-year high combined with declining EPS and flat revenue at all-time-high prices leaves risk-adjusted reward skewed unfavorably despite MCD's high-quality franchise.
key_factors: P/E 31.9x near top of 1y range (26.0-35.8) — valuation stretched
EPS declining: $2.45 Q1'23 vs $2.68 prior-year quarter (-8.6%)
Revenue essentially flat YoY: $5.90B Q1'23 vs $5.93B Q1'22
Stock near all-time highs (+20.9% 1y, +6.7% 3m) — limited margin of safety
Quality franchise intact: 41% operating margin, 29.4% net margin, Dividend Aristocrat

sources_cited.excerpt: Back Near All-Time Highs, Is It Too Late to Buy McDonald's Stock?

UNH.rationale: High-quality compounder (24.8% ROE, ~18% Q1 EPS growth) trading at 21.3x PE near its 1-year low after a mid-June ~7% pullback, with mostly positive news flow offset by one cautionary "trouble" piece.
key_factors: Q1 2023 EPS $5.95 vs $5.03 YoY (~+18%) and revenue $91.93B vs $82.79B (~+11%) — fundamentals accelerating
PE diluted 21.3x near 1-year low of 20.3x (range 20.3-28.9x) — valuation reset, not stretched
ROE 24.8% and gross margin 89.7% — best-in-class profitability
Sharp mid-June drop from $493.62 (6/13) to $458.49 (6/16) on "Is UNH Stock in Trouble?" article, partial recovery to $480.64 — entry-point opportunity with residual headline risk
News flow mostly positive (AI winners, cash-generating machines, dividend growth, Optum Rx, home healthcare expansion) — sentiment skews constructive
